specific_concerns: Mapping 'unborn, eternal, ever-existing, ancient' (applied in the verse to the imperishable embodied / Self) to 'the customer-need predates your codebase' compresses Brahman/Self-grade eternality into 'older-than-tenure' antiquity. A practitioner could read this as flattening the metaphysical 'unborn' into ordinary historical precedence. The engineering layer mitigates by (a) preserving literal_meaning and traditional_meaning_consensus, (b) explicitly tagging the analog as 'four years young as code, fifty years young as cryptography, two thousand years young as a human-coordination problem' — finite numbers, not infinite — and (c) naming the counter-example honestly when engineering work pushes genuinely new substrates.
verdict_reason: Trivialization risk is real but managed. The engineering layer never claims the customer-need IS metaphysically eternal in Shankara's sense; it claims the lineage of the customer-need has a temporal-precedence structure that can be read alongside the verse's temporal-modification denial. The Sanskrit four-predicate sequence is preserved verbatim and not laundered. PASS at severity 2.
